svg.text: Scheduled C2C Flow — Cron Execution Lifecycle
1. ProcessFlow(key, "", flow, false) — creates handler, NO execute
Result: venv IS PERSISTENT — created once, reused by every cron tick
"*/5 * * * * * *" (7 fields) → trims last 2 chars → "*/5 * * * * *" (6 fields: robfig/cron with seconds)
level=info msg="Cron job for flow 3650 is scheduled successfully, entry-id in cron instance: 1, schedule expression: */5 * * * * *"
PHASE 2 — Cron Execution Cycle (repeats every 5 seconds)
level=info "Executing cron job for flow-id: 3650, schedule expression: */5 * * * * *"
• Only Python process is new each tick
PHASE 3 — Health Monitor (persistent, separate from cron)
• Spawns ONE persistent Python process with --health-monitor true
Python Health Monitor Process (PERSISTENT — does NOT exit)
TIP: To verify cron is firing, grep for Go-level log: kubectl logs <pod> | grep "Executing cron job"
COMPLETE PROCESS MAP — All Processes Inside the Pod
Go Process (PID 1) — soar-light binary — PERSISTENT (lives for entire pod lifetime)
• Parses FLOW_JSON • Creates venv • Registers cron • Manages handlers • Runs LogReceiver (captures Python stdout) • Exposes :8081 health + :9152 metrics
Cron instance: robfig/cron/v3 with Second-precision parser → fires callback every N seconds → each callback calls ExecuteJob()
Runs: while True → get_health_status() → sleep(300) → loop
Python Cron Jobs — SHORT-LIVED (new PID each tick)
Health Monitor — always running (checks every 5 min)
Process Count at any given time:
1 Go process + 1 Health Python + 0-1 Cron Python = 2-3 processes
Created ONCE at startup. Destroyed ONLY when flow is deleted.
